items.q: How is the dependency ratio calculated?
items.correct: (Population aged 0-14 plus population aged 65+) divided by population aged 15-64, multiplied by 100 — comparing dependants to the working-age population.
items.misconception: The dependency ratio is the number of children per family.
items.random_wrong: The dependency ratio is the number of unemployed people in a country.
items.why: It compares life-stage groups, not employment status. High ratios can come from many children (LICs) OR many elderly (HICs) — both create burden but very different policy challenges.

items.q: Why do meanders migrate across a floodplain?
items.correct: Faster water on the outside of the bend causes erosion (river cliff); slower water on the inside causes deposition (slip-off slope) — over time the channel shifts laterally.
items.misconception: Meanders form because rivers flow around obstacles like rocks.
items.random_wrong: Meanders form because the river is going downhill very steeply.
items.why: Meanders form on gentle gradients, not steep ones, and self-organise rather than dodging obstacles. Students project an intuitive 'water goes around things' model onto a fluid dynamic process.

items.q: What is a positive climate feedback loop?
items.correct: A process where warming triggers changes that cause more warming — e.g. melting Arctic ice exposes dark ocean, which absorbs more heat, melting more ice (ice-albedo feedback).
items.misconception: Positive feedback means a good outcome for the climate.
items.random_wrong: Positive feedback means the climate balances itself out.
items.why: 'Positive' here means amplifying, not beneficial. Negative feedbacks (which stabilise) are positive for us. The terminology trips students up — it refers to direction of change, not desirability.

items.q: Who coined the concept of 'soft power'?
items.why: Correct answer: Joseph Nye. “Edward Said” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.
Correct answer: Gender Inequality Index. “Global Income Index” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: In which year did Joseph Nye first popularise 'soft power' in a book?
items.why: The real figure is 1990. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
